<sentence>The risotto begins with a foundational layer of finely diced aromatics, typically onions and garlic, sweated until translucent.</sentence>

<sentence>This patient addition and stirring create a creamy, emulsified texture without the need for external cream.</sentence>
<sentence>As the cooking progresses, the rice grains swell and become tender, transitioning from a firm bite to a yielding softness.</sentence>
<sentence>The characteristic texture is a delicate balance between al dente grains and a rich, unctuous sauce.</sentence>
<sentence>Throughout the process, the risotto develops a glossy sheen, a testament to the starch development.</sentence>
<sentence>Towards the end, a final flourish of fat, often butter and Parmesan cheese, is vigorously stirred in, creating the signature "mantecatura."</sentence>
<sentence>This final step binds the ingredients, further enhancing the creaminess and flavor profile.</sentence>
<sentence>The finished risotto should flow gently from the spoon, not be stiff and clumpy, presenting a luxurious and comforting mouthfeel.</sentence>
